_README: Framework metadata and level crosswalk for Brooklyn.
CRITICAL: `verified` is false on every crosswalk below. No official equivalence exists
between WIDA, CA ELD, TX ELPS, and NY levels — the frameworks define proficiency
differently and vendors who claim exact alignment are overstating. These mappings are
a defensible starting point authored from framework structure, NOT from the standards
documents themselves, and they MUST be validated by someone holding each state's current
documents before any of this is shown to a district in that state.
Domain-level mappings are high confidence. Expectation-code-level mappings (e.g. which
specific TX ELPS student expectation a card satisfies) are deliberately ABSENT rather than

wida.name: WIDA ELD Standards Framework
wida.coverage: ~40 states and territories via the WIDA Consortium

standards.SI: Language for Social and Instructional purposes

wida.codeLevelMapping: present — Brooklyn authors directly against WIDA standards + Key Uses

caEld.name: California ELD Standards
caEld.note: Largest single EL population in the US. Not a WIDA state.

structure.partI: Interacting in Meaningful Ways — Collaborative, Interpretive, Productive

caEld.codeLevelMapping: TODO — requires the current CA ELD Standards document. Do not guess.
caEld.stateAdoption: California has a state adoption process for K-8 instructional materials.

txElps.name: Texas English Language Proficiency Standards (ELPS)
txElps.note: Second-largest EL population in the US. Not a WIDA state.

structure.crossCurricular: Learning strategies, Listening, Speaking, Reading, Writing

txElps.codeLevelMapping: TODO — requires the current ELPS text. Do not guess at expectation codes.
txElps.stateAdoption: Texas runs a state review/adoption process for instructional materials.

nyNls.name: New York State Next Generation Learning Standards / ELL supports
nyNls.note: Large EL population. Not a WIDA state.
nyNls.codeLevelMapping: TODO — requires current NYSED documents. Do not guess.
nyNls.privacyNote: NY Ed Law 2-d imposes specific vendor obligations. Legal review required.

levelCrosswalk.caveat: Approximate structural alignment only. Frameworks define proficiency on different constructs; a student placed at one framework's level 2 is not necessarily at another's. Never present this as official equivalence to a district.

1.confidence: high — all four frameworks treat this band as single words, formulaic chunks, and non-verbal response

2.confidence: medium — CA collapses six WIDA levels into three, so this band straddles a CA boundary

3.confidence: medium — TX Advanced spans more than WIDA 3 alone

domainCrosswalk.caveat: Domain alignment is far more stable across frameworks than level alignment. Confidence is high here.
